summary: All spec requirements for scheduler-backends-gcp are fully implemented and verified. R4 (module registration): scheduler/mod.rs adds `#[cfg(feature = "cloud-scheduler")] pub mod cloud_scheduler_backend` and re-exports CloudSchedulerBackend + CloudSchedulerConfig, exactly matching the ion_backend pattern. R5 (trait compatibility): CloudSchedulerBackend implements SchedulerBackend with all 5 methods (acquire_leader, renew_leader, release_leader, get_task_state, set_task_state); backend.rs and TaskScheduleState are unchanged. R6 (feature isolation): cloud-scheduler = ["dep:reqwest"] is independent of scheduler (Ion) and Memory backends; gcp-full convenience alias added. The spec Test Plan section exists but is a placeholder (<!-- TODO -->, 0 test cases); the Hard Reject Rule's second condition is false because the diff contains 13+ #[test]/#[tokio::test] annotations in mod.rs backends_gcp_tests and 45+ in cloud_scheduler_backend_tests.rs.

code_matches_spec.notes: R4: scheduler/mod.rs (diff lines 2821-2831) adds `#[cfg(feature = "cloud-scheduler")] pub mod cloud_scheduler_backend` after ion_backend declaration, and `#[cfg(feature = "cloud-scheduler")] pub use cloud_scheduler_backend::{CloudSchedulerBackend, CloudSchedulerConfig}` after IonSchedulerBackend re-export — follows identical pattern to `#[cfg(feature = "scheduler")] pub mod ion_backend`. lib.rs (diff lines 2807-2808) also re-exports both types behind the feature flag, consistent with the established ion_backend pattern in lib.rs. R5: CloudSchedulerBackend::new() constructs successfully; all 5 trait methods are exercised through Box<dyn SchedulerBackend> in backends_gcp_tests (s3_trait_object_acquire_leader, s3_trait_object_renew_leader, s3_trait_object_release_leader, s3_trait_object_get_task_state, s3_trait_object_set_task_state). backend.rs is absent from the diff — no modifications to SchedulerBackend trait or TaskScheduleState struct. r5_task_schedule_state_unchanged test confirms fields (enabled, last_run_at, total_run_count) and Default behavior are unaffected. R6: Cargo.toml diff (line 74) defines `cloud-scheduler = ["dep:reqwest"]` independently of `scheduler` (Ion) feature and its cclab-kv/deadpool-redis deps. The spec notes that base64 is a direct workspace dep (not optional) so it does not need explicit activation — implementation correctly omits it per spec constraint 'Do NOT duplicate dependency entries'. gcp-full = ["gcp-push", "cloud-scheduler"] convenience feature added at diff line 82.

tests_present.notes: Spec has a ## Test Plan section (scheduler-backends-gcp.md:103-106) but it is an empty placeholder (<!-- TODO -->, 0 test cases defined). The Hard Reject Rule's second condition is false: the diff contains 13 #[test]/#[tokio::test] annotations in scheduler/mod.rs backends_gcp_tests (lines 2853-3101) covering S1 (s1_cloud_scheduler_backend_importable, s1_cloud_scheduler_config_importable, s1_cloud_scheduler_module_reexports), S3 (all 5 trait methods via dyn SchedulerBackend, s3_trait_object_all_methods_in_sequence), S4 (s4_memory_and_cloud_backends_coexist, s4_backends_interchangeable_at_runtime, s4_backends_independent_state), R5 (r5_task_schedule_state_unchanged), R6 (r6_memory_backend_unaffected_by_cloud_feature). Additionally 45+ tests in cloud_scheduler_backend_tests.rs (line 3679+) cover CloudSchedulerBackend internals.

no_regressions.notes: All changes are strictly additive: scheduler/mod.rs only appends new module declaration, re-export, and test module; lib.rs only appends a new cfg-gated re-export block; Cargo.toml only adds new optional deps (reqwest, jsonwebtoken) and new features (cloud-scheduler, gcp-full). No existing files are modified: backend.rs, ion_backend.rs, memory_backend.rs, and the existing scheduler feature configuration are untouched. The new backends_gcp_tests module is guarded by #[cfg(all(test, feature = "cloud-scheduler"))] so it does not affect default test runs.

findings.area: spec quality — Test Plan section is an empty placeholder
findings.issue: scheduler-backends-gcp.md ## Test Plan section contains only <!-- TODO -->
findings.detail: scheduler-backends-gcp.md:103-106 — the Test Plan section header exists with a type annotation but no test cases are defined. The implementation adds 13 well-structured tests in backends_gcp_tests mapped to S1/S3/S4/R5/R6 but this mapping is not formalized in the spec. The companion change specs (cloud-scheduler-backend, broker-traits) share the same gap.
findings.required_fix: Not blocking for implementation approval. Recommend filling in the Test Plan section in a follow-up spec fill cycle to formalize the S1-S4 scenario to test-function mapping.

positive_observations: Module registration in scheduler/mod.rs is a perfect structural match to the ion_backend pattern — same cfg attribute, same position relative to existing declarations, same re-export style
All 4 scenarios (S1-S4) and all 3 requirements (R4-R6) are covered by named, commented test functions that explicitly cite the scenario/requirement they verify
Feature isolation is clean: cloud-scheduler only activates dep:reqwest; no google-cloud-* SDK deps are pulled, no overlap with scheduler (Ion) or default (Memory) dependency graphs
Test s4_backends_independent_state verifies that Memory and Cloud backends maintain truly independent in-memory state — not just type-level coexistence

S2 (feature exclusion) is correctly handled by structural argument: the entire cloud_scheduler_backend module is behind #[cfg(feature = "cloud-scheduler")], so absence of the feature makes the types unavailable at compile time — no runtime test required
gcp-full convenience feature composing gcp-push + cloud-scheduler is a useful ergonomic addition not specified but consistent with existing gcp/gcp-push patterns
